title: Governed agents: Looker + MCP explained
summary: Google Cloud Tech presents an integration of Looker's semantic layer with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Agent Development Kit (ADK) to enable agents that query data through a governed business model rather than generating SQL directly. The approach bridges LLMs with enterprise data while maintaining semantic governance.
writeup: This video shows how to combine Looker's semantic layer, the Model Context Protocol (MCP), and the Agent Development Kit (ADK) to build governed agents. The core idea is grounding LLMs in validated business models instead of letting them generate SQL directly, which keeps queries tied to defined metrics and governance rules. The Looker MCP Server acts as the bridge between LLMs and enterprise data systems. The takeaway is an architecture that constrains agent behavior to a trusted semantic layer rather than raw database access.
